task_name: FES (Fundamentals of Endoscopic Surgery) - General Educational Content
reason: This video is an instructional/educational presentation about the FES program history, structure, validation methodology, and clinical assessment tools (GAGES). It contains no actual performance footage of any FLS/MISTELS task. The frames show: FES program logos and branding, history slides, project timelines, validation statistics, exam information, GAGES scoring methodology, and unrelated imagery (children at beach). No trainee or surgeon is performing any of the five FLS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture).

teaching_content.educational_context: This is a comprehensive overview presentation about the Fundamentals of Endoscopic Surgery (FES) program, including its history, development by SAGES leadership (Lisa Jukelevics, Gerry Fried, Lee Silen, Nat Soper, Lee Swanstrom), official launch in August 2010, validation studies with 720+ test subjects across five skills, and integration with clinical assessment tools like GAGES (Global Assessment of GI Endoscopic Skills). The content addresses program structure as an off-the-shelf baseline proficiency tool for surgical PGY-3 residents and gastroenterology fellows.

frame_notes: Frames 1-8 contain SAGES/FES branding and program overview. Frames 9-15 show project timeline documentation (2006-2010). Frames 16-18 display validation study parameters and sample sizes. Frames 19-20 show GAGES clinical assessment information. Frames 21-22 contain unrelated imagery (beach scene with children) — likely filler or transition content.
conclusion: No FLS task performance to score. Video is purely educational/documentary in nature.
